comment.body:
Alma, concerning your mention of the Athenians in yesterday's chat. I just want to drop a great passage from Thucidydes, Peloponessian War 1,75-76Athenians speak about their own imperialism: ".And the nature of the case first compelled us to advance our empire to its present height; fear being our principal motive, though honor and interest afterwards came in...it was not a very wonderful action, or contrary to the common practice of mankind, if we did accept an empire that was offered to us, and refused to give it up under the pressure of three of the strongest motives, fear, honor, and interest. And it was not we who set the example, for it has always been the law that the weaker should be subject to the stronger. Besides, we believed ourselves to be worthy of our position, and so you thought us till now, when calculations of interest have made you take up the cry of justice—a consideration which no one ever yet brought forward to hinder his ambition when he had a chance of gaining anything by might. And praise is due to all who, if not so superior to human nature as to refuse dominion, yet respect justice more than their position compels them to do."
Fear, honor and interest - the universal motives of empires, plus the eternal "natural" law - weaker should be subject to the stronger. Thucidydes nailed it down 2400 years ago, and we still have not created a political environment to render his observations obsolete, to make them a thing of the past. Sadly.

And it was not we who set the example, for it has always been the law that the weaker should be subject to the stronger. Besides, we believed ourselves to be worthy of our position, and so you thought us till now, when calculations of interest have made you take up the cry of justice—a consideration which no one ever yet brought forward to hinder his ambition when he had a chance of gaining anything by might. And praise is due to all who, if not so superior to human nature as to refuse dominion, yet respect justice more than their position compels them to do."
Fear, honor and interest - the universal motives of empires, plus the eternal "natural" law - weaker should be subject to the stronger. Thucidydes nailed it down 2400 years ago, and we still have not created a political environment to render his observations obsolete, to make them a thing of the past. Sadly.

comment.body:
Hi guys! Good luck for today. One key event to watch is the SMCI 10-K deadline. Nasdaq previously granted SMCI a 180-day extension after their former auditors refused to sign off on the financials—a significant red flag. Retail investors are optimistic that SMCI will meet the deadline and file their 10-K before tonight.
If they fail to do so, they face potential delisting from Nasdaq. A second 180-day extension seems highly unlikely, given their lack of demonstrated goodwill in publishing their financials.
While it's uncertain whether they will meet the deadline or not, a delisting one day before Nvidia’s earnings report—with SMCI being Nvidia’s third-largest client—could create significant market turmoil, especially as investor sentiment toward AI stocks becomes more cautious.
On one hand, waiting until the last possible day to file financials is highly unusual for a listed company. On the other hand, borrow costs and availability for SMCI suggest that major index arbitrage desks, like Citadel, are not heavily shorting the stock... Time will tell

comment.body:
TAIL EVENT:
when market is heavily skewed bullish and when plp aren't leveraged on VIX anymore, dealers aren't hedged against tail risk. to forseeing a tail event look for expansion of size in deep OTM VIX calls, sudden high convexities, margin puts in SPX... so when the momentum in the tail of the distribution curve expands
